defs: The act of offending in any sense; esp., a crime or a sin, an affront or an injury.
quotes: Who was delivered for our offenses, and was raised again for our justification.
I have given my opinion against the authority of two great men, but I hope without offense to their memories.

defs: The state of being offended or displeased; anger; displeasure; as, to cause offense.
quotes: He was content to give them just cause of offense, when they had power to make just revenge.

defs: A cause or occasion of stumbling or of sin.
quotes: Woe to that man by whom the offense cometh!

defs: In any contest, the act or process of attacking as contrasted with the act of defending; the offensive; as, to go on the offense.
The members of a team who have the primary responsibility to score goals, in contrast to those who have the responsibility to defend, i.e. to prevent the opposing team from scoring goal.
notes: ☞ This word, like expense, is often spelled with a c. It ought, however, to undergo the same change with expense, the reasons being the same, namely, that s must be used in offensive as in expensive, and is found in the Latin offensio, and the French offense.
